title: Horror Films Dominate Oscars 2026
text: Horror achieved significant recognition at the 98th Academy Awards ceremony held March 15, 2026, with three major productions taking home multiple awards. Sinners (Ryan Coogler) led horror's victories with four Oscars, including Best Actor for Michael B. Jordan, Best Original Screenplay, Best Original Score, and Best Cinematography, garnering a record 16 nominations. Frankenstein (Guillermo del Toro) triumphed in technical categories, winning Best Costume Design, Best Makeup and Hairstyling, and Best Production Design, with the makeup award going to Mike Hill, Jordan Samuel, and Cliona Furey. Weapons (Zac Cregger) earned Best Supporting Actress for Amy Madigan's performance. Despite losing major categories like Best Picture to One Battle After Another, Sinners' unprecedented nomination count represents the strongest recognition of horror since The Silence of the Lambs in 1991, demonstrating the Academy's growing appreciation for the genre.
